content: University SIS enrollment flows typically model registration as a state machine: Enrolled (successful), Waitlisted (with position number, auto-promotes when a seat opens), Dropped (student-initiated during add/drop, no permanent record), Withdrawn (after add/drop deadline, records a W grade, does not vanish from history), and administrative rejection/error states (e.g. prerequisite not met, hold on account) that block enrollment before it completes. Enrollment attempts either succeed atomically or fail with a specific reason surfaced to the student (closed, prereq, hold, time conflict).
